title: Improve location picking with search and reusable locations
author: bhujelaayushgc
body:
### Pre-submission Checklist

- [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this has not already been requested
- [x] This is a feature request, not a bug report or support question

### Area

Web UI, API, Memo editor, Search / filters, Resources / attachments

### Problem or Use Case

Finding a location is difficult when browser location permission is unavailable or intentionally disabled. Without it, users must drag the map from its default position or manually enter latitude and longitude values.

Enabling browser location is not a complete solution. The location attached to a memo may differ from the user's current location. A memo could refer to a previously visited place, an upcoming destination, or somewhere associated with its subject.

Locations are also commonly reused across memos. Currently, users must find and enter the same location again for every memo.

### Who Would Use This?

This would help users who:

- Do not want to grant browser location permission.
- Create memos about locations other than their current location.
- Frequently create memos from or about the same places.
- Prefer searching by a familiar place name instead of entering coordinates.

### Proposed Solution

Add location search to the memo location picker.

Selecting a search suggestion should move the map to that area without automatically selecting a location. The user can then adjust the map and explicitly choose the desired point.

The picker should also display locations previously used in the authenticated user's memos:

- Show the five most recently used locations initially.
- Provide an option to show more.
- Preserve user-defined friendly names when locations are reused.
- Keep saved locations separate from search suggestions.

Similar to tags, locations can remain part of memo payloads and be derived for reuse instead of introducing a separate locations table.

### Alternatives Considered

- **Enable browser location:** This requires permission and only provides the device's current location, which may not be the location the memo is about.
- **Drag the map manually:** This is slow when the map starts far from the desired location (I am from Nepal and it defaults to Paris).
- **Enter latitude and longitude:** This requires finding coordinates elsewhere (Google Maps) and because the Lat & Lng is separate, pasting the coordinate is not intuitive for most users.
- **Add a separate locations table:** This would provide explicit persistence but introduces additional schema and synchronization requirements. Deriving reusable locations from memo payloads follows the existing tag-style approach.

### Compatibility and Migration

This should not affect existing memos because locations remain stored in their existing memo payloads.

The API addition would be additive and authenticated. Users should only be able to list locations derived from their own memos.

No database schema change or migration should be required.

### Examples

_No response_

### Additional Context

Search results should only reposition the map, not automatically attach a location to the memo. This keeps location selection explicit.

I have a working implementation and can open a pull request after receiving feedback on the proposed direction.

- #1826 and #2903 introduced the ability to attach and display memo locations, but do not cover searching for or reusing locations.
- #4762 includes a request for location search because manually navigating the map is difficult, but the issue focused on configurable map providers and was closed without implementing search.
- #5813 proposed configurable default coordinates as another way to reduce manual map navigation, but was closed as not planned.

I could not find an existing request for reusing locations from previous memos or preserving their user-defined friendly names.

title: relations property missing from webhook payload on new comments
author: Bottleface
body:
### Pre-submission Checklist

- [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this has not already been reported
- [x] I have reproduced this bug on the latest Memos release, current `main`, or the demo site, or this report is specifically about upgrading to the latest release
- [x] This is a bug report, not a question or configuration request

### Affected Area

Other

### Reproduced On

Latest release

### Memos Version Tested

Version: 0.29.1 Commit: 5f194da7d3f71a428b437898cdfd073c66a3df38

### Previous Version

_No response_

### Environment Details

- Deployment: Docker
- Database: SQLite
- Browser: Edge/Brave (not sure the browser matters for this one)
- Server OS: DSM 7.3.2-86009 Update 4
- Reverse Proxy: nginx
- Related Settings: Webhooks

### Reproduction Frequency

Always

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Get a webhook url on https://webhook.site/
2. Add the webhook in Memos
3. Create a memo
4. Comment on that memo

### Current Behavior

When a comment is created the following two events come through the webhook:

- memos.memo.created
- memos.memo.comment.created

Neither webhook contains the `relations` property in the JSON payload.  I don't know if this is intentional or if the webhook is being sent before the relations property on the memo is populated.

I know that the `relations` property is eventually populated because if I edit or delete that comment, I get a fully populated `relations` property in the resulting JSON body of the `updated` or `deleted` webhook events.

On a separate but related note, reactions don't trigger a `memo.updated` event.  I feel like they should, but don't know if this omission was intentional or not.  If a 'deleted' or 'updated' event comes through after a reaction has been made, the property is filled out.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/565a3fd2-06fe-4f99-995c-91124a3bf82c

### Expected Behavior

I would expect that the `relations` property for a comment should be populated in the JSON payloads for the `created` and `comment.created` webhook events.

I would also expect a reaction to trigger an `updated` event.

### Regression Information

I've only been using memos for a few days so I don't know if these are regressions or if they ever worked like I am expecting they should.

### Logs

```shell

```

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: Apostrophe in tag name
author: sergeolkhovik
body: ### Pre-submission Checklist - [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this has not already been reported - [x] I have reproduced this bug on the latest Memos release, current `main`, or the demo site, or this report is specifically about upgrading to the latest release - [x] This is a bug report, not a question or configuration request ### Affected Area Web UI ### Reproduced On Latest release ### Memos Version Tested neosmemo/memos:stable (docker), Version: 0.29.1 ### Previous Version _No response_ ### Environment Details Server: Ubuntu 26.04 + nginx + docker + mariadb, client: Chrome 149.0.7827.116 on MacOS M1 26.5. The problem is that in Ukrainian language apostrophe symbol is widely used, for example: сімʼя (family). And when I type such word as a tag name (i.e. #сім'я) then only part of the word becomes a tag: Happens both via WebUI and memogram (0.5.0). ### Reproduction Frequency Always ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Add new memo (or edit existing one) 2. Type some text and put some tags in Ukrainian including apostrophe (#сімʼя): Це буде тест #сім'я 3. Hit Ctrl-Enter to save the note 4. Only '#сім' will become a tag. ### Current Behavior On saving the content with tag incuding apostrophe only part of the text becomes a tag. This appears both in the note and in the tags list. ### Expected Behavior The entire text (i.e. "#сімʼя") should be a tag. ### Regression Information _No response_ ### Logs ```shell ``` ### Additional Context _No response_

title: Caret is transparent when clicking in empty space in the editor
author: codayon
body:
### Pre-submission Checklist

- [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this has not already been reported
- [x] I have reproduced this bug on the latest Memos release, current `main`, or the demo site, or this report is specifically about upgrading to the latest release
- [x] This is a bug report, not a question or configuration request

### Affected Area

Web UI, Markdown / editor

### Reproduced On

Latest release

### Memos Version Tested

Version: canary Commit: dc41480ece7653a4c91142211999a3fd284aa6f5

### Previous Version

_No response_

### Environment Details

- Deployment: Docker
- Host OS: Arch Linux
- Browser: Zen Browser (Firefox) and Brave Origin (Chromium)

### Reproduction Frequency

Always

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open the memo editor.
2. Click inside the editor in an empty area where there is no nearby text.
3. Observe that no caret is visible.
4. Move the cursor next to existing text.
5. Observe that the caret becomes visible.

### Current Behavior

The editor has focus, but the insertion caret is not rendered when positioned in empty space.

### Expected Behavior

The caret should remain visible whenever the editor has focus, regardless of whether it is positioned next to existing text or in an empty area.

### Regression Information

_No response_

### Logs

```shell

```

### Additional Context

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/42976b6b-1fbd-4d83-9254-18f7af8c8b01

title: Improve username validation error messages
author: codayon
body:
### Pre-submission Checklist

- [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this has not already been reported
- [x] I have reproduced this bug on the latest Memos release, current `main`, or the demo site, or this report is specifically about upgrading to the latest release
- [x] This is a bug report, not a question or configuration request

### Affected Area

Other

### Reproduced On

Latest release

### Memos Version Tested

Version: canary Commit: dc41480ece7653a4c91142211999a3fd284aa6f5

### Previous Version

_No response_

### Environment Details

- Deployment: Docker
- Host OS: Arch Linux

### Reproduction Frequency

Always

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open the sign-up page.
2. Enter a username such as word.word.
3. Submit the form.

### Current Behavior

The client toast displays:

```txt
[invalid_argument] rpc error: code = InvalidArgument desc = invalid username: word.word
```

### Expected Behavior

The error should explain the validation rule that was violated. For example:

`Username can only contain letters, numbers, and hyphens.`
`Username cannot consist only of numbers.`
`Username must start and end with a letter or number.`

Alternatively, if there are multiple validation rules, the API could return a structured validation error that the frontend can present in a user-friendly way.

### Regression Information

_No response_

### Logs

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: [Feature Request]: Editing Tags in one go
author: raviwarrier
body:
### Pre-submission Checklist

- [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this has not already been requested
- [x] This is a feature request, not a bug report or support question

### Area

Tags / organization

### Problem or Use Case

When I started using Memos I wasn't so diligent with how I used tags. But as my usage grew I created a system for how tags were to be used - rules like when to use hyphens or underscores, when to capitalize, etc.

The problem today is that I have some tags that do not follow my rules because when I first started using them I didn't have them.

For example, I have #name-of-person and I have 60+ notes with that tag. If I want to change it to #Name-of-person (capitalize first letter), I have to manually edit all the notes with that tag.

another example is #playinggames. I want it to be just #game now because when I type something like "Bought Assassin's Creed Shadows" #playinggames doesn't make sense, but #game would, i.e., "Bought Assassin's Creed Shadows #game" and "Played Assassin's Creed Shadows #game". 

I have about 10-12 tags that require such edits. 

It would be nice to have a tag editor in Settings > Tags to change the tag label which automatically reflects in all the relevant posts.



### Who Would Use This?

Anyone who wants to change tags or the way they organize their notes or who want to re-organize their notes

### Proposed Solution

Mentioned above

### Alternatives Considered

_No response_

### Compatibility and Migration

_No response_

### Examples

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: Private instance mode: redirect anonymous users away from Explore
author: axsuul
body:
Note: this issue body follows the Feature Request template fields.

### Pre-submission Checklist

- [X] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this has not already been requested
- [X] This is a feature request, not a bug report or support question

### Area

Web UI, Authentication / SSO, Administration

### Problem or Use Case

I run Memos as a private instance. I still want it reachable on the public internet, but anonymous visitors should not see the Explore page.

Today `/` redirects logged-out users to `/explore`. That is surprising for private instances. It also means if I accidentally create a public memo, it can show up to anyone who visits the instance.

Memos used to have `disallow_public_visibility`, which also redirected logged-out users to `/auth`. That was removed in #5743, but there is still a real private-instance use case that is not covered by share links.

### Who Would Use This?

People running personal, family, or team-only Memos instances.

This is especially useful for single-user instances and SSO-backed deployments where Memos is public on the internet but intended only for authenticated users.

### Proposed Solution

Add a private instance setting that makes unauthenticated users redirect to `/auth` instead of `/explore`.

Possible behavior:

- When enabled, anonymous visits to `/` go to `/auth`.
- Anonymous visits to `/explore` go to `/auth`.
- Authenticated users can still use `/explore`.
- Existing share links can keep working if maintainers want public shares to remain accessible.
- This should be separate from "disable creating public memos" if those are considered different concepts.

### Alternatives Considered

Reverse proxy redirects can hide `/explore`, but they only check cookies and cannot reliably know whether the Memos session is valid.

Custom JavaScript injection was suggested in #6023, but it is a workaround and not safe as an access control mechanism.

A fork can restore the old behavior, but this seems useful enough to support as an official private-instance mode.

### Compatibility and Migration

Default behavior should stay the same for existing public instances.

This could be opt-in, for example a new admin setting under System or Memo settings. Existing instances would not change unless the admin enables private instance mode.

### Examples

Related issues and PRs:

- #4406 asked for `/auth` instead of `/explore` on private instances.
- #5743 removed `disallow_public_visibility`.
- #6023 discusses the missing option in 0.29.1.
- The old behavior was referenced in 5686fdf, where `disallowPublicVisibility` redirected anonymous users to `/auth`.

### Additional Context

The core request is not only "disable public memo creation." It is "hide the instance from anonymous visitors." A private instance should not show Explore unless the visitor is authenticated.

title: Mermaid diagrams need zoom, pan, and fullscreen controls
author: cbingb666
body:
## Area
Web UI

## Problem or Use Case
Currently, Mermaid diagrams rendered in memos lack interactive controls. When viewing a complex diagram (e.g., large flowcharts, detailed sequence diagrams, architecture diagrams), users cannot:

1. **Zoom in/out** — large diagrams with many nodes become unreadable on any screen size without the ability to zoom
2. **Pan/move** — once zoomed in (even via browser zoom), there is no way to drag and pan around the diagram to inspect different areas
3. **Fullscreen view** — there is no way to view the diagram in a dedicated fullscreen mode for detailed inspection

The current implementation (`MermaidBlock.tsx`) simply renders the SVG inside a `overflow-x-auto` container with horizontal scrolling only, which is insufficient for large 2D diagrams that stretch both horizontally and vertically.

Related: #5180 addressed the sizing issue but did not add interactive controls. #5616 requests similar zoom controls for images — a unified approach could benefit both.

## Who Would Use This?
- Anyone documenting system architecture, workflows, or complex processes using Mermaid in memos
- Users viewing shared memos with technical diagrams on any device
- Particularly painful on mobile/tablet where the viewport is small and diagrams are scaled down even further

## Proposed Solution
Add interactive controls to the Mermaid block when rendered:

1. **Click to open fullscreen modal** — clicking on the diagram opens it in an overlay/dialog at full viewport size
2. **Zoom controls** — mouse wheel / pinch-to-zoom support, with on-screen +/- buttons as a fallback
3. **Pan/drag** — when zoomed in, allow click-and-drag to pan around the diagram, or in fullscreen mode
4. **Reset zoom button** — restore to fit-to-container view

Ideally this could reuse or align with whatever zoom/pan component is built for images (see #5616).

## Examples
Here is the current render code:

```tsx
<div
  className={cn("mermaid-diagram w-full flex justify-center items-center my-2 overflow-x-auto", className)}
  dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: svg }}
/>
```

A possible improvement could look like:

```tsx
<MermaidViewer svg={svg}>
  {/* click → fullscreen dialog with zoom + pan */}
  {/* mouse wheel → zoom */}
  {/* drag → pan when zoomed */}
  {/* double-click → fit to screen */}
</MermaidViewer>
```

Reference implementations from other tools:
- GitHub renders Mermaid with click-to-open lightbox and zoom support
- Docusaurus uses `@docusaurus/theme-mermaid` with built-in zoom
- Excalidraw-style pan/zoom behavior in diagram viewers

## Additional Context
The Mermaid library itself supports interactive features — this is purely a frontend rendering enhancement in the Memos web UI. No backend or API changes needed.

title: Max. of 10 comments shown when `View all` is clicked
author: bdalenoord
body: ### Pre-submission Checklist - [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this has not already been reported - [x] I have reproduced this bug on the latest Memos release, current `main`, or the demo site, or this report is specifically about upgrading to the latest release - [x] This is a bug report, not a question or configuration request ### Affected Area Web UI ### Reproduced On Latest release ### Memos Version Tested 0.29.1 ### Previous Version _No response_ ### Environment Details - Deployment: Docker - Database: SQLite - Browser: Firefox on both Android and Ubuntu - Server OS: Debian 6 - Reverse proxy: NGINX ### Reproduction Frequency Always ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Create a new note 2. Open the note, click `Write a comment` and type `1` as a comment 3. Repeat that until you reach 11 (or more, doesnt matter as long as it is over 10) 4. Only the 10 most recent comments are shown, and the count indicates there are only 10 5. Navigate back to the note overview, notice that on the line that indicates the comments it does in fact state `Comments (11)` now, so somewhere the system knows there are more than 10 comments, there is however no way to see them: ### Current Behavior Only the 10 most recent comments are shown, and the comment count differs between the note overview and the note detail. ### Expected Behavior All comments to be shown, either immediately or via something like a `load more`. Also, the counters should match and both show the correct amount of > 10 notes ### Regression Information _No response_ ### Logs ```shell ``` ### Additional Context _No response_

title: Failed to start up with rootless docker and MEMOS_UID=0
author: adoyle-h
body:
### Pre-submission Checklist

- [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this has not already been reported
- [x] I have reproduced this bug on the latest Memos release, current `main`, or the demo site, or this report is specifically about upgrading to the latest release
- [x] This is a bug report, not a question or configuration request

### Affected Area

Deployment / startup

### Reproduced On

Latest release

### Memos Version Tested

v0.29.1

### Previous Version

_No response_

### Environment Details

rootless docker v29.1.3
arch linux

### Reproduction Frequency

Always

### Steps to Reproduce

docker run -d \
  --name memos \
  -p 5230:5230 \
  -v ~/.memos:/var/opt/memos \
  -e MEMOS_UID=0 \
  -e MEMOS_GID=0 \
  neosmemo/memos:stable

### Current Behavior

Memos failed to start up without any logs. 

### Expected Behavior

Memos start up without error.

### Additional Context

I run all containers in rootless docker. Because of userns-remap, the UID=1000/GID=1000 would be remapped to UID=100999/GID=100999 on host.
I want the data folder (~/.memos) keep UID=1000/GID=1000. So it should be `-e MEMOS_UID=0 -e MEMOS_GID=0`.
But, it will enter the infinite `exec` loop in memos/scripts/entrypoint.sh.
https://github.com/usememos/memos/blob/ba609c5cb807960b700a5fa0b814559b752f7335/scripts/entrypoint.sh#L9-L15

title: Document Kubernetes installation with the HelmForge Helm chart
author: mberlofa
body:
Hi maintainers,

I was looking through the project documentation and did not find a Kubernetes installation section using Helm. If I missed an existing official page, please point me to it and feel free to close this.

HelmForge now publishes a production-oriented Helm chart for Memos:

- Documentation: https://helmforge.dev/docs/charts/memos
- Chart source: https://github.com/helmforgedev/charts/tree/main/charts/memos

The basic installation flow is intentionally simple:

~~~bash
helm repo add helmforge https://repo.helmforge.dev
helm repo update
helm install memos helmforge/memos --namespace memos --create-namespace
~~~

The chart is meant to provide a Kubernetes-native path for users who want to run Memos on their own clusters, with explicit values, pinned upstream images, and common production surfaces such as persistence, service exposure, probes, security contexts, and optional integrations where applicable.

Would you be open to adding a short Kubernetes / Helm installation section to the documentation that references this chart as a community-maintained deployment option?

If that direction makes sense, I would be happy to prepare a documentation PR with a concise install section, links to the HelmForge chart documentation, and any wording you prefer around support boundaries.

title: MCP: Add create_attachment tool for uploading files
author: cbingb666
body:
### Pre-submission Checklist

- [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this has not already been requested
- [x] This is a feature request, not a bug report or support question

### Area

Resources / attachments, API

---

## Problem / Use Case

The built-in MCP server for Memos already exposes tools to manage attachments — **list, get, delete, and link** — but lacks an **upload** tool. This gap prevents AI assistants and automation from:

- Attaching screenshots generated during automated workflows
- Uploading PDFs, images, or other files alongside created memos
- Completing end-to-end note‑taking scenarios that involve file attachments

Currently, the only workaround is to bypass MCP entirely and call the REST API (`POST /api/v1/attachments` with `multipart/form-data`) directly, which requires separate authentication and undermines the single‑interface promise of MCP.

---

## Who Would Use This?

- AI agent users creating memos with file attachments via MCP
- Automation workflows that generate screenshots, exports, or reports and need to store them in Memos
- Anyone using Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, Zed, Hermes, etc. with the Memos MCP server

---

## Proposed Solution: Two-Step Upload with One-Time Token

**Do NOT pass file content through MCP tool arguments.** Embedding base64-encoded binary in tool calls causes context explosion for AI agents (base64 inflates data by 33%; a 500 KB screenshot becomes ~670 KB of text — roughly 160K+ tokens — and 10 MB files become completely unmanageable at ~3.3M tokens).

Instead, use a **credential-separation pattern**: the MCP tool returns a one-time upload URL, and the agent performs the actual binary transfer via a standard HTTP call outside the MCP channel.

### Step 1: `create_attachment` (MCP tool — lightweight)

Returns an upload credential. No file content in the MCP channel.

```json
{
  "name": "create_attachment",
  "description": "Create an attachment and get a one-time upload URL. The agent must then POST the file binary to the returned upload_url via curl or HTTP client.",
  "inputSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "filename": { "type": "string", "description": "File name (e.g., screenshot.png)" },
      "mime_type": { "type": "string", "description": "MIME type (e.g., image/png, application/pdf)" },
      "memo": { "type": "string", "description": "Optional: memo resource name to link to immediately (format: memos/{uid})" }
    },
    "required": ["filename", "mime_type"]
  }
}
```

**Response:**

```json
{
  "attachment_name": "attachments/abc123",
  "upload_url": "https://memos.example.com/api/v1/attachments/upload?token=eyJ...",
  "expires_in": 300
}
```

### Step 2: Binary upload (standard HTTP, outside MCP)

The agent uses `curl` or any HTTP client to POST the actual file. Zero context overhead, no size limits beyond server storage.

```bash
curl -F "file=@screenshot.png" "https://memos.example.com/api/v1/attachments/upload?token=eyJ..."
```

### Server-side requirements

One new endpoint: `POST /api/v1/attachments/upload?token=<one-time-token>`

- Accepts `multipart/form-data` with a `file` field (identical to the existing attachment upload)
- Token is single-use, short-lived (5 minutes), and scoped to the pre-created attachment record
- Reuses the existing attachment storage backend — no new storage logic needed
- Token can be a signed JWT or a random opaque string stored alongside the pending attachment record

### Full Agent Workflow Example

```
User: "Save this screenshot as a memo with tags."

Agent:
  1. MCP call: create_attachment(filename="screenshot.png", mime_type="image/png")
     → { attachment_name: "attachments/def456", upload_url: "https://...?token=xxx" }

  2. Terminal: curl -F "file=@screenshot.png" "https://...?token=xxx"
     → 200 OK (10 MB binary uploaded directly, zero MCP context cost)

  3. MCP call: create_memo(content="Screenshot #b/cbing #d/Tools ...", ...)
     → Done
```

---

## Why Not Base64 Inline?

Passing base64 content through MCP tool arguments **fails catastrophically for real-world file sizes**:

| File size | Base64 size | Approx. tokens | Viable? |
|-----------|------------|----------------|---------|
| 100 KB | 133 KB | ~33K | Marginal |
| 500 KB | 667 KB | ~167K | High risk |
| 1 MB | 1.33 MB | ~333K | No — blows context |
| 10 MB | 13.3 MB | ~3.3M | Impossible |

Real-world uploads (screenshots, PDFs, audio) routinely exceed 1 MB. The two-step token approach handles files of any size with zero context overhead.

---

## Alternatives Considered

1. **Base64 inline via MCP** — simplest to implement but causes context explosion for files >100 KB; rejected as non-viable for real-world use.
2. **Call REST API directly** — works but requires separate auth and bypasses MCP.
3. **MCP resource/Uri reference** — pass a `file://` path and have the server read it; not viable since MCP servers cannot access agent filesystems.

---

## Compatibility & Migration

- **Additive only** — a new tool and endpoint; no changes to existing tools or schemas.
- The new `POST /api/v1/attachments/upload?token=` endpoint reuses the existing attachment storage backend.
- The optional `memo` parameter allows immediate linking or standalone uploads.
- No database migration required — tokens can be stored in-memory or in a lightweight table alongside pending attachments.
- Existing MCP clients and REST API consumers remain unaffected.

title: [Feature Request] Add UI buttons
author: Justinzobel
body:
### Pre-submission Checklist

- [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this has not already been requested
- [x] This is a feature request, not a bug report or support question

### Area

Web UI

### Problem or Use Case

Rather than having to click on the 3 dots, move the mouse, click again to action an item.

### Who Would Use This?

Me :)

### Proposed Solution

I'd like to be able to have buttons for archive/delete, etc shown in the note area so I can quickly action notes.

This could be off by default to preserve the current experience and available to toggle on in preferences/settings either per user or per instance.

### Alternatives Considered

Buttons that only show on hover.

### Compatibility and Migration

Off by default so no compatibility/migration needed.

### Examples

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: Footnote formatting
author: patricksthannon
body: ### Pre-submission Checklist - [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this has not already been requested - [x] This is a feature request, not a bug report or support question ### Area Memo editor ### Problem or Use Case Memos recognizes footnotes formatting, as it hides the footnote, but doesn't implement it properly in a saved note. Currently you can add a footnote in markdown in edit mode and it will disappear when the memo is saved - but it is still saved within the memo, if you edit again it will appear. If you copy that saved memo, the footnote will appear in your copy. Example input: ``` This is a sentence that requires a citation^[1]. [^1]: This is the footnote text appearing at the bottom of the page. ``` Example Saved Output: ``` This is a sentence that requires a citation^[1]. ``` ### Who Would Use This? Like most people who use markdown, footnotes provide source citations or to add extra commentary to the bottom of a note without disrupting the flow of the main text. ### Proposed Solution Implement a footnote enhancement so memos properly formats them correctly. Footnote links would be a nice addition as well. ``` This is a sentence that requires a citation^[1]. This is an additional sentence. This is a third sentence. ------------------------------------------------------------------- [^1]: This is the footnote text appearing at the bottom of the page. ``` ### Alternatives Considered _No response_ ### Compatibility and Migration _No response_ ### Examples ### Additional Context _No response_

title: Masonry layout for wide screens
author: lukas346
body:
### Pre-submission Checklist

- [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this has not already been requested
- [x] This is a feature request, not a bug report or support question

### Area

Web UI

### Problem or Use Case

The single-column view is great for narrow screens like smartphones, but on wider 16:9 displays it leaves unused space that could be filled more efficiently. A manual or automatic masonry layout for notes would make better use of the available screen space.

### Who Would Use This?

Wide screen app users.

### Proposed Solution

Manual option:
* add switch; column/masonry layout

or

Auto option:
* base on screen width automatically switch between column layout and masonry

### Alternatives Considered

_No response_

### Compatibility and Migration

_No response_

### Examples

![](https://external-content.duckduckgo.com/iu/?u=https%3A%2F%2Fstatic1.makeuseofimages.com%2Fwordpress%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2018%2F08%2FGoogleKeepWebInterface.jpg&f=1&nofb=1&ipt=497b28a584f0266dbdf2d5264046e1d998e9c2fa33e3398e5ae060cd171f409f)

### Additional Context

_No response_

title: MCP: List/search tools return arrays in structuredContent, causing MCP validation errors
author: Cbrogg
body:
### Pre-submission Checklist

- [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this has not already been reported
- [x] I have reproduced this bug on the latest Memos release, current `main`, or the demo site, or this report is specifically about upgrading to the latest release
- [x] This is a bug report, not a question or configuration request

### Affected Area

API

### Reproduced On

Latest release

### Memos Version Tested

neosmemo/memos:stable

### Previous Version

_No response_

### Environment Details

- Memos version: v0.29.1
- Built-in MCP endpoint
- Authentication: OAuth
- Tested clients:
  - ChatGPT MCP Connector
  - LiteLLM MCP Playground
  - Postman (direct MCP requests)

### Reproduction Frequency

Always

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create two memos.
2. Create a relation between them.
3. Call:
```json
{
  "method": "tools/call",
  "params": {
    "name": "list_memo_relations",
    "arguments": {
      "name": "memos/<memo-id>",
      "type": "REFERENCE"
    }
  }
}
```

### Current Behavior

The tool executes successfully, but the MCP response fails validation.
Example error:
```
Invalid input: expected record, received array

path:
structuredContent
```
LiteLLM:
```
ValidationError: Input should be a valid dictionary
input_type=list
```
ChatGPT Connector reports the same validation error.
Postman reproduces the same behaviour when talking directly to the built-in MCP endpoint.

### Expected Behavior

The tool should return valid MCP structuredContent.
Current behaviour appears equivalent to:
```json
{
  "structuredContent": [
    {
      "memo": "...",
      "related_memo": "...",
      "type": "REFERENCE"
    }
  ]
}
```
Instead, structuredContent should be an object, for example:
```json
{
  "structuredContent": {
    "relations": [
      {
        "memo": "...",
        "related_memo": "...",
        "type": "REFERENCE"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```
The exact field name (relations, items, etc.) is not important.
The important part is that structuredContent is an object rather than a raw array.


### Regression Information

_No response_

### Logs

```shell

```

### Additional Context

## Additional observations
This appears to affect only tools returning collections.
Single-object responses work correctly.
Because the same error is reproducible with ChatGPT, LiteLLM and direct Postman requests against the MCP endpoint, the issue appears to originate from the built-in MCP implementation rather than from individual MCP clients.

## Possible root cause
It looks like array responses are written directly into structuredContent.
Something conceptually similar to:
structuredContent = result // result is []T
instead of wrapping the array inside an object before returning the MCP response.
